I Started Building It to Practice Python. I Finished It Understanding What Real Software Development Actually Feels Like.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Brecht Corbeel on UnsplashWhen I first started learning Python, I believed programming was about writing code.
The more code you wrote…
The better programmer you became.
At least, that’s what I thought.
So every day I watched tutorials.
Copied examples.
Solved beginner exercises.
Built tiny calculators.
Made number guessing games.
Printed colorful text in the terminal.
It felt like progress.
But deep down, something was missing.
I knew Python syntax.
I knew what loops did.
I understood functions.
Yet if someone had asked me to build something useful from scratch…
I wouldn’t have known where to begin.
That realization was frustrating.
